source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/2023 시험소 규격업데이트/A1417, A1717, F1417/Safety 접수자료 231218/3. Risk management/A-TCF-5-104 Risk Management Report (Rev.2 230915).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk acceptability, risk control, overall residual risk review, and production/post-production information for the device.
keyPoints: The device is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, not for mammography.
Applicable regulations listed are Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
The report applies EN ISO 14971:2019 and references related safety, EMC, software, usability, labeling, QMS, biological evaluation, and post-market guidance documents.
Before risk control, the report lists 2 acceptable and 44 unacceptable risk-region entries; after risk control, it lists 44 acceptable and 0 unacceptable entries.
Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities... assignment of responsibilities and authorities... requirements for review of risk management activities...

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Contraindications
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography and direct cardiac application. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance. - Acquisition image is obtained as intended.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Risk control option analysis
concepts.evidence: We used one or more of the following risk control options in priority order listed below... inherently safe design and manufacture... protective measures... information for safety...

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After performing risk control measures, evaluate and record residual risks according to the criteria defined in the risk management plan.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: Appropriate methods to obtain relevant production and post-production information Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance...

concepts.name: Adverse event reporting
concepts.evidence: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency...

evidence.claim: Patient contact is with the front of the detector for less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The device contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.19 Does the medical device contain software? A The product contains software.

evidence.claim: The detector can connect to a PC via Ethernet and Wi-Fi.
evidence.evidenceText: Power Supply Box(or Power Adapter & Ethernet cable) can be connected to the PC with an Ethernet Cable. The detector can be connected to a PC via Wi-Fi.

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability criteria define risk levels 1 to 4 as acceptable and 5 to 25 as unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 44 risks are in the acceptable region and none are in the unacceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results after risk control... EN ISO 14971:2019 44 0 44

evidence.claim: All risks are controlled by adequate risk control measures in accordance with the risk management plan.
evidence.evidenceText: All risks are controlled by adequate risk control measures in accordance with risk management plan.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information is collected through quality assurance, R&D, production, management, and quality manager activities.
evidence.evidenceText: Department Information collecting and review in production and post-production Quality Assurance... R&D... Production... Management... Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata signals FDA, but the source text lists European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Republic of Korea Medical Device Act; no FDA authority is present in the provided source text.
Risk count evidence is internally inconsistent: the narrative states 40 risks were identified, while risk evaluation tables and totals report 44 risks.

OCR or source text quality issues are present, including misspellings such as 'corraective' and 'vaildation' and possible standard number inconsistencies such as 'EN 15322-1' where other sections list EN 15223-1.
Document number A-TCF-5-104 is visible in source path and cross-references, but no explicit document number field appears in the document title block.
